587.reason: [When a lexical error occurs, the program should NOT output to lexer.txt; it should only write the error to error.txt. Additionally, m_nTokenType may be uninitialized or invalid at this point, leading to incorrect or undefined behavior when indexing g_strTokenCategoryCode.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [When a lexical error occurs, the program should NOT output to lexer.txt; it should only write the error to error.txt. Additionally, m_nTokenType may be uninitialized or invalid at this point, leading to incorrect or undefined behavior when indexing g_strTokenCategoryCode.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [When a lexical error occurs, the program should only output to error.txt, not lexer.txt. Additionally, m_nTokenType may be uninitialized or invalid at this point, leading to incorrect or undefined behavior when indexing g_strTokenCategoryCode.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [When a lexical error occurs, the program should NOT output to lexer.txt; it should only output the error line and code to error.txt. Additionally, m_nTokenType may be uninitialized or invalid at this point, leading to undefined behavior or wrong token category output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [When a lexical error is caught, the program should NOT write to lexer.txt; it should only write the error info to error.txt. Additionally, lexicalAnalyzer.GetTokenType() is invalid in this context because no valid token was produced.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [When a lexical error occurs, the program should NOT output to lexer.txt; it should only output the error to error.txt. Additionally, m_nTokenType may be uninitialized or invalid at this point, leading to incorrect or undefined behavior when indexing g_strTokenCategoryCode.]
